prd.title: MyContext AI CLI: The Living Brain for Spec-Driven Development
prd.problemStatement: Developers, especially those leveraging AI, spend significant time bootstrapping projects, managing context, and ensuring consistency across the codebase. AI code generation often lacks deep project awareness, leading to generic, disconnected, or incorrect outputs. There is a critical need for a system that maintains a dynamic, 'living' understanding of a project, which can be used by both humans and AI agents to build, evolve, and test applications with high fidelity and speed.
goals: To provide a CLI tool that can initialize, analyze, and manage a 'Living Brain' (a comprehensive JSON context file) for any web project.
To orchestrate a suite of specialized AI agents that can read from and write to the Living Brain to perform tasks like code generation, documentation, visual testing, and refactoring.
To enable a spec-driven development workflow where changes to the spec (the Living Brain) can be used to automatically generate or update code, tests, and documentation.
To integrate multi-modal AI (vision) for UI analysis, automated testing, and demo creation, bridging the gap between design and implementation.
To provide robust project analysis and planning tools that generate accurate, context-aware build strategies and executable plans.
prd.targetAudience: Software developers, development teams, and AI engineers building modern web applications, with a primary focus on the Next.js/React ecosystem.
successMetrics: Reduction in project setup and scaffolding time by over 50%.
Achieve 90%+ accuracy for AI-generated components based on a well-defined Living Brain context.
Adoption of the CLI tool in developer workflows, measured by active projects and command usage.
Number of successful, end-to-end 'build-app' workflow executions from a simple prompt.
Positive developer feedback on the quality and contextual relevance of generated code and artifacts.

features.name: Living Brain Management
features.description: Core functionality to initialize, analyze, and manage the project's 'Living Brain' (context.json). This includes generating context from scratch, analyzing existing projects, and validating context integrity.
features.userValue: Provides a single source of truth for the project, ensuring that both developers and AI agents have a consistent and deep understanding of the project's requirements, architecture, and state.
acceptanceCriteria: The `init` command creates a .mycontext directory with a basic context structure.
The `analyze` command scans an existing project and populates the Living Brain with inferred details.
The `generate context` command uses AI to flesh out a complete set of context documents (PRD, specs, etc.).
The `validate` command checks the context for completeness and consistency.

features.name: Agent Orchestration System
features.description: A sophisticated system for managing and coordinating a team of specialized AI sub-agents. The orchestrator can execute agents sequentially, in parallel, or in complex workflows with validation gates.
features.userValue: Enables complex, multi-step tasks to be automated reliably. It breaks down large problems into manageable pieces for specialized agents, improving the quality and reliability of the final output.
acceptanceCriteria: The system can register and execute individual agents.
The `WorkflowAgent` can execute a predefined sequence of agents to build an application.
The system supports inter-agent communication via the `AgentCommunicationManager`.
Workflows can handle retries and failures gracefully.

features.name: AI-Powered Code Generation
features.description: Generates production-ready code for components, server actions, hooks, and types based on the Living Brain. It specializes in creating shadcn/ui-based React components for Next.js.
features.userValue: Drastically accelerates development by automating the creation of boilerplate and complex UI components, ensuring they are consistent with the project's design system and technical specifications.
acceptanceCriteria: The `generate-components` command can create single components, groups, or all components defined in the context.
Generated components are type-safe and use TypeScript.
Generated code adheres to best practices for the specified framework (Next.js App Router).
The `BackendDevAgent` can generate server actions and custom hooks for data fetching and mutations.

features.name: Vision-Based Testing & Demos
features.description: An autonomous testing suite that uses vision AI to navigate and validate web UIs like a human user. It can execute test missions, perform visual regression checks, and record professional demos.
features.userValue: Automates end-to-end testing in a way that closely mimics real user interaction, catching visual bugs and usability issues that traditional tests miss. It also automates the creation of marketing and documentation assets.
acceptanceCriteria: The `VisionNavigatorAgent` can follow a multi-step mission on a live web page.
The `VisualValidatorAgent` can compare a screenshot against a baseline and identify significant differences.
The `DemoRecorderAgent` can generate video (MP4), screenshot sequences, and HTML replays of a test run.
The entire workflow is coordinated by the `VisionTestCoordinator`.

features.name: Iterative Enhancement & Refinement
features.description: Allows developers to refine and refactor existing components using natural language prompts. The `EnhancementAgent` analyzes the code and applies the requested changes intelligently.
features.userValue: Streamlines the process of making changes and improvements to the codebase, enabling rapid prototyping and iteration without manual refactoring.
acceptanceCriteria: The `enhance` command can take a component file and a prompt as input.
The agent can add new functionality, refactor logic, or change styling based on the prompt.
The agent provides a diff of the changes before applying them.

flows.name: New Project Initialization Workflow
flows.description: The end-to-end flow for a developer starting a new project from a simple description.
steps: Developer runs `mycontext init-interactive` and provides a one-sentence description of the app.
The system decomposes the request and uses AI to infer a complete project specification (ASL), prompting the user only for low-confidence items.
Upon approval, the system saves the specification (`asl.json`).
The `WorkflowAgent` orchestrates the `ProjectSetupAgent` to scaffold the Next.js project.
The `CodeGenSubAgent` and `BackendDevAgent` are run to generate all components, actions, and hooks defined in the spec.
The project is ready for the developer to run `pnpm dev`.

flows.name: Autonomous Vision-Based UI Test Flow
flows.description: A developer triggers an autonomous UI test on a deployed application.
steps: Developer defines a test mission in a config file (e.g., 'Sign up as a new user and create a new post').
The `VisionNavigatorAgent` opens a browser, navigates to the app URL, and uses vision AI to find and interact with elements (e.g., clicks 'Sign Up', fills form fields).
At key steps, the `VisualValidatorAgent` takes screenshots and compares them against baseline images to check for visual regressions.
Upon completion, the coordinator aggregates the results, including pass/fail status, visual diffs, and a link to the generated demo video.
The results are written to the Living Brain.

edgeCases.description: An AI agent may 'hallucinate' and generate code or text that is incorrect, irrelevant, or nonsensical.
edgeCases.mitigation: Each agent's output is passed through a validation step (e.g., `QASubAgent` for code, schema validation for JSON). Workflows include retry logic. The system uses multiple AI providers and can fallback if one fails.

edgeCases.description: The Living Brain (context.json) may become out of sync with the actual codebase if developers make manual changes without updating the context.
edgeCases.mitigation: The `analyze` command can be run to re-scan the project and suggest updates to the Living Brain. Git hooks can be configured to remind developers to update the context.

edgeCases.description: An automated workflow, especially one with retry logic, could enter an infinite loop if an agent consistently fails in a specific way.
edgeCases.mitigation: Workflows have a maximum attempt limit (`maxRetries`). The `SubAgentOrchestrator` tracks execution time and will time out long-running steps.

designPrinciples: Developer-First: The UI and UX should be intuitive and efficient for developers.
Clarity over Cuteness: Prioritize clear information hierarchy and readability.
Deterministic & Reliable: Processes should be repeatable and produce consistent results.
Extensible: The architecture should allow for new agents and capabilities to be added easily.

entities.name: SubAgent
entities.description: Represents a specialized AI agent within the system.

entities.name: LivingContext
entities.description: The core 'Living Brain' data structure that holds all project context.

entities.name: VisionTestMission
entities.description: Defines a task for the vision-based testing agents to perform.

shared.name: CommandOptions

specs.architecture: The project is a monorepo managed by pnpm workspaces. The primary application is a command-line interface (CLI) tool located in `apps/cli`. This CLI is built with TypeScript and Node.js, using `commander.js` for command parsing. The core logic is architected around a system of specialized AI 'Sub-Agents', managed by a central `SubAgentOrchestrator`. Each agent is a class responsible for a specific domain (e.g., code generation, security, QA). Agents communicate and share state through a central 'Living Brain' (a structured JSON file), following a blackboard architectural pattern. The monorepo also contains several experimental Next.js applications used for testing, demonstration, and as targets for the CLI's capabilities.

projectConfig.name: MyContext AI CLI: The Living Brain for Spec-Driven Development
projectConfig.description: Developers, especially those leveraging AI, spend significant time bootstrapping projects, managing context, and ensuring consistency across the codebase. AI code generation often lacks deep project awareness, leading to generic, disconnected, or incorrect outputs. There is a critical need for a system that maintains a dynamic, 'living' understanding of a project, which can be used by both humans and AI agents to build, evolve, and test applications with high fidelity and speed.

visionDecision.reasoning: The mission explicitly states to 'scroll to see the features' before clicking 'Start Narrative'. The current DOM context and screenshot show only the top portion of the landing page, indicating there are more features below the fold that need to be viewed first.
visionDecision.visualContext: The current view displays the main hero section, a 'Login' button, a 'Start Narrative' button (top right), and the main heading. It's clear there's more content below based on typical landing page structures and the instruction to 'scroll to see features'.

visionDecision.reasoning: The mission requires me to 'explore the landing page, scroll to see the features'. The 'Previous Steps Taken' indicates one scroll action has already occurred. Looking at the current screenshot, there are still sections like 'The Full Content Ecosystem' and a final 'Stop fighting execution...' call to action at the bottom that haven't been fully explored. To ensure all features are seen as per the mission, another scroll down is necessary before clicking 'Start Narrative'.
